options.c(3) Library Functions Manual options.c(3)

NAME

options.c - ⚙️ Main entry point for unified options parsing with mode detection

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct binary_level_opts_t
Create a new options_t struct with all defaults set.

Functions


bool has_action_flag (void)
Check if an action flag was detected. options_t options_t_new (void)
options_t options_t_new_preserve_binary (const options_t *source)
Create new options struct, preserving binary-level fields from source. asciichat_error_t options_init (int argc, char **argv)

Detailed Description

⚙️ Main entry point for unified options parsing with mode detection

Purpose: Implements options_init() - the unified entry point that:

1.
Detects mode from command-line arguments (server, client, mirror, acds, etc.)
2.
Parses binary-level options (--help, --version, --log-file, etc.)
3.
Routes to mode-specific parsing using preset configurations
4.
Loads configuration files if --config specified
5.
Validates all options with cross-field checks
6.
Publishes options via RCU for lock-free thread-safe access

Architecture:

The parsing flow is:

options_init(argc, argv)


  ├─ Mode Detection


  │  └─ Checks for --help, --version, mode keywords, session strings


  ├─ Binary-Level Option Parsing


  │  └─ Handles: --help, --version, --log-file, --verbose, --quiet, etc.


  ├─ Get Mode-Specific Preset (unified)


  │  └─ Uses options_preset_unified() from presets.h


  ├─ Mode-Specific Option Parsing


  │  └─ Builder parses remaining args for the detected mode


  ├─ Configuration File Loading (if --config specified)


  │  └─ Loads and merges TOML config file


  ├─ Validation


  │  └─ Applies defaults, validates ranges, cross-field checks


  └─ RCU Publishing


     └─ Makes options available via options_get() and GET_OPTION()

Key Components:

  • detect_mode_and_parse_binary_options(): Mode detection and binary-level parsing
  • options_preset_unified(): Preset config for all modes (from presets.h)
  • options_config_parse_args(): Builder-based parsing of mode-specific options
  • Configuration file loading (if --config specified)
  • options_state_init() and options_state_set(): RCU publishing

Option Lifecycle in options_init():

1.
Create default options via options_t_new()
2.
Detect mode from argv
3.
Parse binary-level options (may exit for --help, --version)
4.
Get mode-specific preset from registry
5.
Parse mode-specific options into options struct
6.
Load config file if specified
7.
Validate all options with defaults
8.
Initialize RCU state
9.
Publish options for lock-free access

Error Handling:

  • Invalid options → ERROR_USAGE (usage already printed to stderr)
  • File errors → ERROR_IO or ERROR_CONFIG
  • Validation failures → ERROR_VALIDATION with context message
  • Success → ASCIICHAT_OK

Thread Safety:

  • options_init() must be called exactly once before worker threads
  • After options_init() completes, options are read-only
  • Access via lock-free GET_OPTION() macro from worker threads
  • Optional updates via options_set_*() functions

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

January 2026

Definition in file lib/options/options.c.

  options_init (int argc, char ** argv)

849                                                       {
850   log_debug("options_init: starting with argc=%d, argv[0]=%s, argv[1]=%s", argc, argv[0], argc > 1 ? argv[1] : "N/A");
851   // NOTE: --grep filter is initialized in main.c BEFORE any logging starts
852   // This allows ALL logs (including from shared_init) to be filtered
853   // Validate arguments (safety check for tests)
854   if (argc < 0 || argc > 128) {
855     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid argc: %d", argc);
856   }
857   if (argv == NULL) {
858     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "argv is NULL");
859   }
860   // Validate all argv elements are non-NULL up to argc
861   for (int i = 0; i < argc; i++) {
862     if (argv[i] == NULL) {
863       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "argv[%d] is NULL (argc=%d)", i, argc);
864     }
865   }
866   // Initialize RCU options system (must be done before any threads start)
867   // This must happen FIRST, before any other initialization
868   asciichat_error_t rcu_init_result = options_state_init();
869   if (rcu_init_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
870     return rcu_init_result;
871   }
872 
873   // ========================================================================
874   // STAGE 1: Mode Detection and Binary-Level Option Handling
875   // ========================================================================
876 
877   // Check for binary-level actions FIRST (before mode detection)
878   // These actions may take arguments, so we need to check them before mode detection
879   bool show_version = false;
880   bool create_config = false;
881   bool create_manpage = false;
882   bool has_action = false; // Track if any action flag is present
883   const char *config_create_path = NULL;
884   const char *manpage_create_path = NULL;
885 
886   // ========================================================================
887   // STAGE 1A: Quick scan for action flags FIRST (they bypass mode detection)
888   // ========================================================================
889   // Quick scan for action flags (they may have arguments)
890   // This must happen BEFORE logging initialization so we can suppress logs before shared_init()
891   // Also scan for --quiet / -q so we can suppress logging from the start
892   // Also scan for --color early so it affects help output colors
893   bool user_quiet = false;
894   int parsed_color_setting = COLOR_SETTING_AUTO; // Store parsed color value until opts is created
895   bool color_setting_found = false;
896   bool check_update_flag_seen = false;
897   bool no_check_update_flag_seen = false;
898 
899   // FIRST: Scan entire argv for --help (special case - works before OR after mode)
900   for (int i = 1; i < argc; i++) {
901     if (strcmp(argv[i], "--help") == 0 || strcmp(argv[i], "-h") == 0) {
902       // Scan backwards to find if a mode was specified before --help
903       asciichat_mode_t help_mode = MODE_DISCOVERY; // Default to discovery mode (binary-level help)
904       for (int j = i - 1; j >= 1; j--) {
905         if (argv[j][0] != '-') {
906           if (strcmp(argv[j], "server") == 0) {
907             help_mode = MODE_SERVER;
908           } else if (strcmp(argv[j], "client") == 0) {
909             help_mode = MODE_CLIENT;
910           } else if (strcmp(argv[j], "mirror") == 0) {
911             help_mode = MODE_MIRROR;
912           } else if (strcmp(argv[j], "discovery-service") == 0) {
913             help_mode = MODE_DISCOVERY_SERVICE;
914           } else if (strcmp(argv[j], "discovery") == 0) {
915             help_mode = MODE_DISCOVERY;
916           }
917           break; // Found first non-flag argument
918         }
919       }
920 
921       // Show help for the detected mode (or binary-level if no mode)
922       usage(stdout, help_mode);
923       fflush(NULL);
924       _Exit(0);
925     }
926   }
927 
928   // THEN: Scan for other binary-level actions (stops at mode name)
929   for (int i = 1; i < argc; i++) {
930     // Stop scanning at mode name - binary-level options must come before the mode
931     if (argv[i][0] != '-') {
932       bool is_mode =
933           (strcmp(argv[i], "server") == 0 || strcmp(argv[i], "client") == 0 || strcmp(argv[i], "mirror") == 0 ||
934            strcmp(argv[i], "discovery") == 0 || strcmp(argv[i], "discovery-service") == 0);
935       if (is_mode) {
936         break; // Stop processing at mode name
937       }
938     }
939     if (argv[i][0] == '-') {
940       // Handle --quiet, -q, --quiet=value formats
941       bool temp_quiet = false;
942       if (parse_binary_bool_arg(argv[i], &temp_quiet, "quiet", 'q')) {
943         if (temp_quiet) {
944           user_quiet = true;
945         }
946       }
947       // Validate --log-level and --log-file require arguments
948       if (strcmp(argv[i], "--log-level") == 0) {
949         if (i + 1 >= argc || argv[i + 1][0] == '-') {
950           log_plain_stderr("Error: --log-level requires a value (dev, debug, info, warn, error, fatal)");
951           return ERROR_USAGE;
952         }
953         i++; // Skip the argument in this loop
954       }
955       if (strcmp(argv[i], "-L") == 0 || strcmp(argv[i], "--log-file") == 0) {
956         if (i + 1 >= argc || argv[i + 1][0] == '-') {
957           log_plain_stderr("Error: %s requires a file path", argv[i]);
958           return ERROR_USAGE;
959         }
960         i++; // Skip the argument in this loop
961       }
962       // --help is handled in the first scan loop above (works before OR after mode)
963       // Parse --color early so it affects help output colors
964       if (strcmp(argv[i], "--color") == 0) {
965         if (i + 1 < argc && argv[i + 1][0] != '-') {
966           // Check if next arg is a mode name (not a color value)
967           const char *next_arg = argv[i + 1];
968           bool is_mode =
969               (strcmp(next_arg, "server") == 0 || strcmp(next_arg, "client") == 0 || strcmp(next_arg, "mirror") == 0 ||
970                strcmp(next_arg, "discovery") == 0 || strcmp(next_arg, "discovery-service") == 0);
971 
972           if (is_mode) {
973             // Next arg is a mode, not a color value - default to true
974             parsed_color_setting = COLOR_SETTING_TRUE;
975             color_setting_found = true;
976           } else {
977             // Try to parse as color value
978             char *error_msg = NULL;
979             if (parse_color_setting(next_arg, &parsed_color_setting, &error_msg)) {
980               color_setting_found = true;
981               i++; // Skip the setting argument
982             } else {
983               if (error_msg) {
984                 log_error("Error parsing --color: %s", error_msg);
985                 free(error_msg);
986               }
987             }
988           }
989         } else {
990           // --color without argument defaults to true (enable colors)
991           parsed_color_setting = COLOR_SETTING_TRUE;
992           color_setting_found = true;
993         }
994       }
995       // Check for --color=value format
996       if (strncmp(argv[i], "--color=", 8) == 0) {
997         const char *value = argv[i] + 8;
998         char *error_msg = NULL;
999         if (parse_color_setting(value, &parsed_color_setting, &error_msg)) {
1000           color_setting_found = true;
1001         } else {
1002           if (error_msg) {
1003             log_error("Error parsing --color: %s", error_msg);
1004             free(error_msg);
1005           }
1006         }
1007       }
1008       if (strcmp(argv[i], "--version") == 0 || strcmp(argv[i], "-v") == 0) {
1009         show_version = true;
1010         has_action = true;
1011         break;
1012       }
1013       if (strcmp(argv[i], "--check-update") == 0) {
1014         check_update_flag_seen = true;
1015         if (no_check_update_flag_seen) {
1016           log_plain_stderr("Error: Cannot specify both --check-update and --no-check-update");
1017           return ERROR_USAGE;
1018         }
1019         has_action = true;
1020         action_check_update_immediate();
1021         // action_check_update_immediate() calls _Exit(), so we don't reach here
1022         break;
1023       }
1024       if (strcmp(argv[i], "--no-check-update") == 0) {
1025         no_check_update_flag_seen = true;
1026         if (check_update_flag_seen) {
1027           log_plain_stderr("Error: Cannot specify both --check-update and --no-check-update");
1028           return ERROR_USAGE;
1029         }
1030         // Flag will be parsed normally later
1031       }
1032       if (strcmp(argv[i], "--config-create") == 0) {
1033         create_config = true;
1034         has_action = true;
1035         // Check for optional [FILE] argument
1036         if (i + 1 < argc && argv[i + 1][0] != '-') {
1037           config_create_path = argv[i + 1];
1038           i++; // Consume the file path argument
1039         }
1040         break;
1041       }
1042       if (strcmp(argv[i], "--man-page-create") == 0) {
1043         create_manpage = true;
1044         has_action = true;
1045         // Check for optional [FILE] argument
1046         if (i + 1 < argc && argv[i + 1][0] != '-') {
1047           manpage_create_path = argv[i + 1];
1048           i++; // Consume the file path argument
1049         }
1050         break;
1051       }
1052       if (strcmp(argv[i], "--completions") == 0) {
1053         has_action = true;
1054         // Handle --completions: generate shell completion scripts
1055         if (i + 1 < argc && argv[i + 1][0] != '-') {
1056           const char *shell_name = argv[i + 1];
1057           i++; // Consume shell name
1058 
1059           // Check for optional output file
1060           const char *output_file = NULL;
1061           if (i + 1 < argc && argv[i + 1][0] != '-') {
1062             output_file = argv[i + 1];
1063             i++; // Consume output file
1064           }
1065 
1066           action_completions(shell_name, output_file);
1067           // action_completions() calls _Exit(), so we don't reach here
1068         } else {
1069           log_plain_stderr("Error: --completions requires shell name (bash, fish, zsh, powershell)");
1070           return ERROR_USAGE;
1071         }
1072         break; // Unreachable, but for clarity
1073       }
1074       if (strcmp(argv[i], "--list-webcams") == 0) {
1075         has_action = true;
1076         action_list_webcams();
1077         // action_list_webcams() calls _Exit(), so we don't reach here
1078         break;
1079       }
1080       if (strcmp(argv[i], "--list-microphones") == 0) {
1081         has_action = true;
1082         action_list_microphones();
1083         // action_list_microphones() calls _Exit(), so we don't reach here
1084         break;
1085       }
1086       if (strcmp(argv[i], "--list-speakers") == 0) {
1087         has_action = true;
1088         action_list_speakers();
1089         // action_list_speakers() calls _Exit(), so we don't reach here
1090         break;
1091       }
1092       // Check for --show-capabilities (binary-level action)
1093       if (strcmp(argv[i], "--show-capabilities") == 0) {
1094         has_action = true;
1095         action_show_capabilities_immediate();
1096         // action_show_capabilities_immediate() calls _Exit(), so we don't reach here
1097         break;
1098       }
1099     }
1100   }
1101   // Store action flag globally for use during cleanup
1102   set_action_flag(has_action);
1103   // ========================================================================
1104   // STAGE 1B: DO MODE DETECTION EARLY (needed for log_init)
1105   // ========================================================================
1106   asciichat_mode_t detected_mode = MODE_DISCOVERY; // Default mode
1107   char detected_session_string[SESSION_STRING_BUFFER_SIZE] = {0};
1108   int mode_index = -1;
1109 
1110   asciichat_error_t mode_detect_result =
1111       options_detect_mode(argc, argv, &detected_mode, detected_session_string, &mode_index);
1112   if (mode_detect_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1113     return mode_detect_result;
1114   }
1115 
1116   // VALIDATE: Binary-level options must appear BEFORE the mode
1117   // Check if any binary-level options appear after the mode position
1118   if (mode_index > 0) {
1119     for (int i = mode_index + 1; i < argc; i++) {
1120       if (argv[i][0] == '-') {
1121         bool takes_arg = false;
1122         bool takes_optional_arg = false;
1123 
1124         if (is_binary_level_option_with_args(argv[i], &takes_arg, &takes_optional_arg)) {
1125           return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_USAGE, "Binary-level option '%s' must appear before the mode '%s', not after it",
1126                            argv[i], argv[mode_index]);
1127         }
1128       }
1129     }
1130   }
1131   // ========================================================================
1132   // STAGE 1C: Initialize logging EARLY (before any log_dev calls)
1133   // ========================================================================
1134   // Create local options struct and initialize with defaults
1135   options_t opts = options_t_new(); // Initialize with all defaults
1136   opts.detected_mode = detected_mode;
1137   char *log_filename = options_get_log_filepath(detected_mode, opts);
1138   SAFE_SNPRINTF(opts.log_file, OPTIONS_BUFF_SIZE, "%s", log_filename);
1139   // Note: log_init() is called earlier in asciichat_shared_init() and will be
1140   // reconfigured with the actual log level and file in main.c after options are fully parsed
1141   // NOTE: --color detection now happens in src/main.c BEFORE asciichat_shared_init()
1142   // This ensures g_color_flag_passed and g_color_flag_value are set before any logging.
1143   //
1144   // NOTE: Timer system and shared subsystems are initialized by src/main.c
1145   // via asciichat_shared_init() BEFORE options_init() is called.
1146   // This allows options_init() to use properly configured logging.
1147 
1148   // If an action flag is detected OR user passed --quiet, silence logs for clean output
1149   if (user_quiet || has_action) {
1150     log_set_terminal_output(false); // Suppress console logging for clean action output
1151   }
1152   // Apply parsed color setting from STAGE 1A
1153   if (color_setting_found) {
1154     opts.color = parsed_color_setting;
1155   } else {
1156     opts.color = OPT_COLOR_DEFAULT; // Apply default
1157   }
1158 
1159   // If we found version/config-create/create-manpage, handle them immediately (before mode detection)
1160   if (show_version || create_config || create_manpage) {
1161     if (show_version) {
1162       opts.version = true;
1163       options_state_set(&opts);
1164       return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1165     } else if (create_config) {
1166       // Build the schema first so config_create_default can generate options from it
1167       const options_config_t *unified_config = options_preset_unified(NULL, NULL);
1168       if (unified_config) {
1169         asciichat_error_t schema_build_result = config_schema_build_from_configs(&unified_config, 1);
1170         if (schema_build_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1171           // Schema build failed, but continue anyway
1172           (void)schema_build_result;
1173         }
1174         options_config_destroy(unified_config);
1175       }
1176 
1177       // Call action handler which handles all output and prompts properly
1178       action_create_config(config_create_path);
1179       // action_create_config() calls _Exit(), so we don't reach here
1180     } else if (create_manpage) {
1181       // Call action handler which handles all output and prompts properly
1182       action_create_manpage(manpage_create_path);
1183       // action_create_manpage() calls _Exit(), so we don't reach here
1184     }
1185   }
1186 
1187   // Mode detection and logging already initialized early in STAGE 1B/1C above
1188   // (moved earlier to ensure logging is available before any log_dev() calls)
1189 
1190   // Check for binary-level options that can appear before or after mode
1191   // Search entire argv to find --quiet, --log-file, --log-level, -V, etc.
1192   // These are documented as binary-level options that can appear anywhere
1193   for (int i = 1; i < argc; i++) {
1194     if (argv[i][0] == '-') {
1195       // Handle -V and --verbose (stackable verbosity)
1196       if (strcmp(argv[i], "-V") == 0 || strcmp(argv[i], "--verbose") == 0) {
1197         // Check if next argument is a number (optional argument)
1198         if (i + 1 < argc && argv[i + 1][0] != '-') {
1199           // Try to parse as integer count
1200           char *endptr;
1201           long value = strtol(argv[i + 1], &endptr, 10);
1202           if (*endptr == '\0' && value >= 0 && value <= 100) {
1203             opts.verbose_level = (unsigned short int)value;
1204             i++; // Skip next argument
1205             continue;
1206           }
1207         }
1208         // No valid number, just increment
1209         opts.verbose_level++;
1210       }
1211       // Handle binary-level boolean options using the abstraction function
1212       // -q/--quiet, --json, --log-format-console all use the same parser
1213       if (parse_binary_bool_arg(argv[i], &opts.quiet, "quiet", 'q')) {
1214         continue;
1215       }
1216       if (parse_binary_bool_arg(argv[i], &opts.json, "json", '\0')) {
1217         continue;
1218       }
1219       if (parse_binary_bool_arg(argv[i], &opts.log_format_console_only, "log-format-console", '\0')) {
1220         continue;
1221       }
1222       // Handle --log-level LEVEL (set log threshold)
1223       if (strcmp(argv[i], "--log-level") == 0) {
1224         if (i + 1 >= argc) {
1225           log_plain_stderr("Error: --log-level requires a value (dev, debug, info, warn, error, fatal)");
1226           return ERROR_USAGE;
1227         }
1228         if (argv[i + 1][0] == '-') {
1229           log_plain_stderr("Error: --log-level requires a value (dev, debug, info, warn, error, fatal)");
1230           return ERROR_USAGE;
1231         }
1232         char *error_msg = NULL;
1233         if (parse_log_level(argv[i + 1], &opts.log_level, &error_msg)) {
1234           i++; // Skip the level argument
1235         } else {
1236           if (error_msg) {
1237             log_plain_stderr("Error: %s", error_msg);
1238             free(error_msg);
1239           } else {
1240             log_plain_stderr("Error: invalid log level value: %s", argv[i + 1]);
1241           }
1242           return ERROR_USAGE;
1243         }
1244       }
1245       // Handle -L and --log-file FILE (set log file path)
1246       if ((strcmp(argv[i], "-L") == 0 || strcmp(argv[i], "--log-file") == 0)) {
1247         if (i + 1 >= argc) {
1248           log_plain_stderr("Error: %s requires a file path", argv[i]);
1249           return ERROR_USAGE;
1250         }
1251         if (argv[i + 1][0] == '-') {
1252           log_plain_stderr("Error: %s requires a file path", argv[i]);
1253           return ERROR_USAGE;
1254         }
1255         SAFE_STRNCPY(opts.log_file, argv[i + 1], sizeof(opts.log_file));
1256         i++; // Skip the file argument
1257       }
1258       // NOTE: --color is now parsed in STAGE 1A (early, before help processing)
1259       // to ensure help output colors are applied correctly
1260     }
1261   }
1262 
1263   if (show_version) {
1264     // Show binary-level version from src/main.c
1265     opts.version = true;
1266     options_state_set(&opts);
1267     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1268   }
1269 
1270   if (create_config) {
1271     // Handle --config-create: create default config file and exit
1272     // Use provided path or default to user config location
1273     char config_path[PLATFORM_MAX_PATH_LENGTH];
1274     if (config_create_path) {
1275       SAFE_STRNCPY(config_path, config_create_path, sizeof(config_path));
1276     } else {
1277       // Use default config path: ~/.ascii-chat/config.toml
1278       char *config_dir = get_config_dir();
1279       if (!config_dir) {
1280         log_error("Error: Failed to determine default config directory");
1281         return ERROR_CONFIG;
1282       }
1283       safe_snprintf(config_path, sizeof(config_path), "%sconfig.toml", config_dir);
1284       SAFE_FREE(config_dir);
1285     }
1286 
1287     // Create config with default options
1288     asciichat_error_t result = config_create_default(config_path);
1289     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1290       asciichat_error_context_t err_ctx;
1291       if (HAS_ERRNO(&err_ctx)) {
1292         log_error("Error creating config: %s", err_ctx.context_message);
1293       } else {
1294         log_error("Error: Failed to create config file at %s", config_path);
1295       }
1296       return result;
1297     }
1298 
1299     log_plain("Created default config file at: %s", config_path);
1300     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Return successfully after creating config
1301   }
1302 
1303   if (create_manpage) {
1304     // Handle --create-man-page-template: generate merged man page template
1305     // The .1.in file is the existing template to read from (not the output)
1306     const char *existing_template_path = "share/man/man1/ascii-chat.1.in";
1307     options_config_t *config = options_preset_unified(NULL, NULL);
1308     if (!config) {
1309       log_error("Error: Failed to get binary options config");
1310       return ERROR_MEMORY;
1311     }
1312 
1313     // Generate merged man page from embedded or filesystem resources
1314     // (existing_template_path and manpage_content_file parameters are no longer supported)
1315     asciichat_error_t err = options_config_generate_manpage_merged(config, "ascii-chat", NULL, existing_template_path,
1316                                                                    "Video chat in your terminal");
1317 
1318     options_config_destroy(config);
1319 
1320     if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1321       asciichat_error_context_t err_ctx;
1322       if (HAS_ERRNO(&err_ctx)) {
1323         log_error("%s", err_ctx.context_message);
1324       } else {
1325         log_error("Error: Failed to generate man page");
1326       }
1327       return err;
1328     }
1329 
1330     log_plain("Generated man page: %s", existing_template_path);
1331     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Return successfully after generating man page
1332   }
1333 
1334   // ========================================================================
1335   // STAGE 2: Build argv for mode-specific parsing
1336   // ========================================================================
1337   // If mode was found, build argv with only arguments after the mode
1338   // If mode_index == -1, filter out binary-level options from all arguments
1339   int mode_argc = argc;
1340   char **mode_argv = (char **)argv;
1341   char **allocated_mode_argv = NULL; // Track if we need to free mode_argv
1342 
1343   if (mode_index == -1) {
1344     // No explicit mode - filter binary-level options from entire argv
1345     int max_mode_argc = argc; // Worst case: no binary opts skipped
1346 
1347     if (max_mode_argc > 256) {
1348       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Too many arguments: %d", max_mode_argc);
1349     }
1350 
1351     char **new_mode_argv = SAFE_MALLOC((size_t)(max_mode_argc + 1) * sizeof(char *), char **);
1352     if (!new_mode_argv) {
1353       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate mode_argv");
1354     }
1355     allocated_mode_argv = new_mode_argv;
1356 
1357     new_mode_argv[0] = argv[0]; // Copy program name
1358 
1359     // Copy all args except binary-level options
1360     int new_argv_idx = 1;
1361     for (int i = 1; i < argc; i++) {
1362       bool takes_arg = false;
1363       bool takes_optional_arg = false;
1364 
1365       // Check if this is a binary-level option
1366       if (is_binary_level_option_with_args(argv[i], &takes_arg, &takes_optional_arg)) {
1367         // Skip argument if needed
1368         if (takes_arg && i + 1 < argc) {
1369           i++; // Skip required argument
1370         } else if (takes_optional_arg && i + 1 < argc && argv[i + 1][0] != '-') {
1371           i++; // Skip optional argument
1372         }
1373         continue;
1374       }
1375       // Not a binary option, copy to mode_argv
1376       new_mode_argv[new_argv_idx++] = argv[i];
1377     }
1378 
1379     mode_argc = new_argv_idx;
1380     new_mode_argv[mode_argc] = NULL;
1381     mode_argv = new_mode_argv;
1382   } else if (mode_index != -1) {
1383     // Mode found at position mode_index
1384     // Build new argv: [program_name, args_before_mode (no binary opts)..., args_after_mode...]
1385     // Binary-level options are not passed to mode-specific parsers
1386 
1387     int args_after_mode = argc - mode_index - 1;
1388     // We'll calculate final argc after skipping binary options
1389     int max_mode_argc = 1 + (mode_index - 1) + args_after_mode; // Worst case: no binary opts skipped
1390 
1391     if (max_mode_argc > 256) {
1392       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Too many arguments: %d", max_mode_argc);
1393     }
1394 
1395     // Allocate max_mode_argc+1 to accommodate NULL terminator
1396     char **new_mode_argv = SAFE_MALLOC((size_t)(max_mode_argc + 1) * sizeof(char *), char **);
1397     if (!new_mode_argv) {
1398       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate mode_argv");
1399     }
1400     allocated_mode_argv = new_mode_argv; // Track allocation for cleanup
1401 
1402     // Copy: [program_name, args_before_mode (excluding binary opts)..., args_after_mode...]
1403     if (mode_index == 0) {
1404       // Mode is at argv[0], use "ascii-chat" as program name
1405       new_mode_argv[0] = "ascii-chat";
1406     } else {
1407       new_mode_argv[0] = argv[0];
1408     }
1409 
1410     // Copy args before mode, skipping binary-level options
1411     int new_argv_idx = 1;
1412     for (int i = 1; i < mode_index; i++) {
1413       bool takes_arg = false;
1414       bool takes_optional_arg = false;
1415 
1416       // Check if this is a binary-level option
1417       if (is_binary_level_option_with_args(argv[i], &takes_arg, &takes_optional_arg)) {
1418         // Skip argument if needed
1419         if (takes_arg && i + 1 < mode_index) {
1420           i++; // Skip required argument
1421         } else if (takes_optional_arg && i + 1 < mode_index && argv[i + 1][0] != '-') {
1422           i++; // Skip optional argument
1423         }
1424         continue;
1425       }
1426       // Not a binary option, copy to mode_argv
1427       new_mode_argv[new_argv_idx++] = argv[i];
1428     }
1429     // Copy args after mode, filtering out any binary-level options (they shouldn't appear here)
1430     int args_after_mode_idx = 0;
1431     for (int i = mode_index + 1; i < argc; i++) {
1432       bool takes_arg = false;
1433       bool takes_optional_arg = false;
1434 
1435       // Check if this is a binary-level option (shouldn't appear after mode)
1436       if (is_binary_level_option_with_args(argv[i], &takes_arg, &takes_optional_arg)) {
1437         // Skip binary-level option and its argument if needed
1438         if (takes_arg && i + 1 < argc) {
1439           i++; // Skip required argument
1440         } else if (takes_optional_arg && i + 1 < argc && argv[i + 1][0] != '-') {
1441           i++; // Skip optional argument (note: removed isdigit check for consistency)
1442         }
1443         continue; // Skip this option
1444       }
1445       // Not a binary option, copy to mode_argv
1446       new_mode_argv[new_argv_idx + args_after_mode_idx++] = argv[i];
1447     }
1448     // Calculate actual argc (program + filtered args before + filtered args after)
1449     mode_argc = new_argv_idx + args_after_mode_idx;
1450     new_mode_argv[mode_argc] = NULL;
1451 
1452     mode_argv = new_mode_argv;
1453   }
1454 
1455   // ========================================================================
1456   // STAGE 3: Set Mode-Specific Defaults
1457   // ========================================================================
1458   // Initialize all defaults using options_t_new_preserve_binary() to keep binary-level
1459   // options (--quiet, --verbose, --log-level, etc.) from being reset
1460   opts = options_t_new_preserve_binary(&opts);
1461   // If a session string was detected during mode detection, restore it after options reset
1462   // This ensures discovery mode knows which session to join
1463   if (detected_session_string[0] != '\0') {
1464     SAFE_STRNCPY(opts.session_string, detected_session_string, sizeof(opts.session_string));
1465     log_info("options_init: Detected session string from argv: '%s'", detected_session_string);
1466   }
1467 
1468   // Encryption options default to disabled/empty
1469   opts.no_encrypt = 0;
1470   opts.encrypt_key[0] = '\0';
1471   opts.password[0] = '\0';
1472   opts.encrypt_keyfile[0] = '\0';
1473   opts.server_key[0] = '\0';
1474   opts.client_keys[0] = '\0';
1475   opts.palette_custom[0] = '\0';
1476 
1477   // Set different default addresses for different modes
1478   if (detected_mode == MODE_CLIENT || detected_mode == MODE_MIRROR || detected_mode == MODE_DISCOVERY) {
1479     // Client/Mirror/Discovery: connects to localhost by default (discovery uses ACDS to find server)
1480     SAFE_SNPRINTF(opts.address, OPTIONS_BUFF_SIZE, "localhost");
1481     opts.address6[0] = '\0'; // Client doesn't use address6
1482   } else if (detected_mode == MODE_SERVER) {
1483     // Server: binds to 127.0.0.1 (IPv4) and ::1 (IPv6) by default
1484     SAFE_SNPRINTF(opts.address, OPTIONS_BUFF_SIZE, "127.0.0.1");
1485     // address6 is now a positional argument, not an option
1486     opts.address6[0] = '\0';
1487   } else if (detected_mode == MODE_DISCOVERY_SERVICE) {
1488     // ACDS: binds to all interfaces by default
1489     SAFE_SNPRINTF(opts.address, OPTIONS_BUFF_SIZE, "0.0.0.0");
1490     opts.address6[0] = '\0';
1491   }
1492 
1493   // ========================================================================
1494   // STAGE 4: Build Dynamic Schema from Unified Options Config
1495   // ========================================================================
1496   // Build the config schema dynamically from the unified config
1497   // This generates TOML keys, CLI flags, categories, and types from builder data
1498   const options_config_t *unified_config = options_preset_unified(NULL, NULL);
1499   if (unified_config) {
1500     asciichat_error_t schema_build_result = config_schema_build_from_configs(&unified_config, 1);
1501     if (schema_build_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1502       // Schema build failed, but continue with static schema as fallback
1503       (void)schema_build_result;
1504     } else {
1505     }
1506     options_config_destroy(unified_config);
1507   } else {
1508   }
1509 
1510   // ========================================================================
1511   // STAGE 5: Load Configuration Files
1512   // ========================================================================
1513   // Extract binary-level options BEFORE config loading (config may reset them)
1514   binary_level_opts_t binary_before_config = extract_binary_level(&opts);
1515   // Publish options to RCU before config loading so that config logs get proper colors
1516   // from the parsed --color setting (e.g., --color=true)
1517   asciichat_error_t config_publish_result = options_state_set(&opts);
1518   if (config_publish_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1519   } else {
1520   }
1521   // Load config files - now uses detected_mode directly for bitmask validation
1522   // Save flip_x and flip_y as they should not be reset by config files
1523   // Also save encryption settings - they should only be controlled via CLI, not config file
1524   bool saved_flip_x_from_config = opts.flip_x;
1525   bool saved_flip_y_from_config = opts.flip_y;
1526   bool saved_encrypt_enabled = opts.encrypt_enabled;
1527   asciichat_error_t config_result = config_load_system_and_user(detected_mode, false, &opts);
1528   (void)config_result; // Continue with defaults and CLI parsing regardless of result
1529   // Restore binary-level options (don't let config override command-line options)
1530   restore_binary_level(&opts, &binary_before_config);
1531 
1532   // Restore flip_x and flip_y - config shouldn't override the defaults
1533   opts.flip_x = saved_flip_x_from_config;
1534   opts.flip_y = saved_flip_y_from_config;
1535 
1536   // Restore encrypt_enabled - it should only be set via CLI, not config file
1537   // The config can set key/password which auto-enables encryption, but the encrypt_enabled
1538   // flag itself should stay at its default unless the user explicitly passes --encrypt
1539   opts.encrypt_enabled = saved_encrypt_enabled;
1540 
1541   // ========================================================================
1542   // STAGE 6: Parse Command-Line Arguments (Unified)
1543   // ========================================================================
1544   // Extract binary-level options BEFORE applying unified defaults
1545   // (which might override them from config files)
1546   binary_level_opts_t binary_before_defaults = extract_binary_level(&opts);
1547   asciichat_mode_t mode_saved_for_parsing = detected_mode; // CRITICAL: Save before defaults reset
1548   // Get unified config
1549   options_config_t *config = options_preset_unified(NULL, NULL);
1550   if (!config) {
1551     SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
1552     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to create options configuration");
1553   }
1554   int remaining_argc;
1555   char **remaining_argv;
1556 
1557   // Save flip_x and flip_y before applying defaults (should not be reset by defaults)
1558   bool saved_flip_x = opts.flip_x;
1559   bool saved_flip_y = opts.flip_y;
1560   // Apply defaults from unified config
1561   asciichat_error_t defaults_result = options_config_set_defaults(config, &opts);
1562   if (defaults_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1563     options_config_destroy(config);
1564     SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
1565     return defaults_result;
1566   }
1567   // Restore binary-level options (they should never be overridden by defaults)
1568   restore_binary_level(&opts, &binary_before_defaults);
1569 
1570   // Restore flip_x and flip_y - they should keep the values from options_t_new()
1571   // unless explicitly set by the user (but defaults shouldn't override them)
1572   opts.flip_x = saved_flip_x;
1573   opts.flip_y = saved_flip_y;
1574 
1575   // Restore detected_mode before parsing so mode validation works.
1576   opts.detected_mode = mode_saved_for_parsing;
1577 
1578   // Save flip_x and flip_y before parsing - they should not be reset by the parser
1579   bool saved_flip_x_for_parse = opts.flip_x;
1580   bool saved_flip_y_for_parse = opts.flip_y;
1581   // Note: json is already saved via extract_binary_level/restore_binary_level mechanism
1582   // Parse mode-specific arguments
1583   option_mode_bitmask_t mode_bitmask = (1 << mode_saved_for_parsing);
1584   log_debug("About to parse options_config with mode_bitmask=%u, mode_argc=%d", mode_bitmask, mode_argc);
1585   asciichat_error_t result =
1586       options_config_parse(config, mode_argc, mode_argv, &opts, mode_bitmask, &remaining_argc, &remaining_argv);
1587   log_debug("options_config_parse returned: %d", result);
1588   // Restore flip_x and flip_y - they should keep their values unless explicitly overridden
1589   opts.flip_x = saved_flip_x_for_parse;
1590   opts.flip_y = saved_flip_y_for_parse;
1591   // json is already restored via the call to options_state_set which calls restore_binary_level
1592   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1593     options_config_destroy(config);
1594     SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
1595     // Convert ERROR_CONFIG to ERROR_USAGE for command-line parsing errors
1596     if (result == ERROR_CONFIG) {
1597       asciichat_error_context_t err_ctx;
1598       const char *error_detail = "invalid options";
1599       if (HAS_ERRNO(&err_ctx)) {
1600         error_detail = err_ctx.context_message;
1601       }
1602       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_USAGE, "Failed to parse options: %s", error_detail);
1603     }
1604     return result;
1605   }
1606 
1607   // ========================================================================
1608   // STAGE 6.5: Publish Parsed Options Early
1609   // ========================================================================
1610   // Publish options to RCU as soon as they're parsed
1611   // This ensures GET_OPTION() works during cleanup even if validation fails
1612   log_debug("Publishing parsed options to RCU before validation");
1613   asciichat_error_t early_publish = options_state_set(&opts);
1614   if (early_publish != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1615     log_error("Failed to publish parsed options to RCU state early");
1616     options_config_destroy(config);
1617     SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
1618     return early_publish;
1619   }
1620   log_debug("Successfully published options to RCU");
1621 
1622   // Auto-enable custom palette if palette-chars was set
1623   if (opts.palette_custom[0] != '\0') {
1624     // palette-chars was set - always use PALETTE_CUSTOM (overrides any explicit --palette setting)
1625     opts.palette_type = PALETTE_CUSTOM;
1626     opts.palette_custom_set = true;
1627     log_debug("Set PALETTE_CUSTOM because --palette-chars was provided");
1628 
1629     // Validate palette characters for UTF-8 correctness
1630     if (!utf8_is_valid(opts.palette_custom)) {
1631       log_error("Error: --palette-chars contains invalid UTF-8 sequences");
1632       options_config_destroy(config);
1633       SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
1634       return option_error_invalid();
1635     }
1636 
1637     // Check if palette contains non-ASCII characters
1638     bool has_non_ascii = !utf8_is_ascii_only(opts.palette_custom);
1639     if (has_non_ascii) {
1640       // Non-ASCII characters require UTF-8 support
1641       // Check if UTF-8 is explicitly disabled or unavailable
1642       bool utf8_disabled = (opts.force_utf8 == COLOR_SETTING_FALSE);
1643       bool utf8_auto_unavailable = (opts.force_utf8 == COLOR_SETTING_AUTO && !terminal_supports_utf8());
1644 
1645       if (utf8_disabled) {
1646         log_error("Error: --palette-chars contains non-ASCII characters but --utf8=false was specified");
1647         log_error("       Remove --utf8=false or use ASCII-only palette characters");
1648         options_config_destroy(config);
1649         SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
1650         return option_error_invalid();
1651       }
1652 
1653       if (utf8_auto_unavailable) {
1654         log_error("Error: --palette-chars contains non-ASCII characters but terminal does not support UTF-8");
1655         log_error("       Use --utf8=true to force UTF-8 mode or use ASCII-only palette characters");
1656         options_config_destroy(config);
1657         SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
1658         return option_error_invalid();
1659       }
1660     }
1661   }
1662 
1663   // Auto-enable encryption if key was provided
1664   if (opts.encrypt_key[0] != '\0') {
1665     // Validate key file exists (skip for remote/virtual keys)
1666     if (!is_remote_key_path(opts.encrypt_key)) {
1667       struct stat st;
1668       if (stat(opts.encrypt_key, &st) != 0) {
1669         log_error("Key file not found: %s", opts.encrypt_key);
1670         options_config_destroy(config);
1671         SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
1672         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Key file not found: %s", opts.encrypt_key);
1673       }
1674       if ((st.st_mode & S_IFMT) != S_IFREG) {
1675         log_error("Key path is not a regular file: %s", opts.encrypt_key);
1676         options_config_destroy(config);
1677         SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
1678         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Key path is not a regular file: %s", opts.encrypt_key);
1679       }
1680     }
1681     opts.encrypt_enabled = 1;
1682     log_debug("Auto-enabled encryption because --key was provided");
1683   }
1684 
1685   // Color filter validation and auto-enable
1686   if (opts.color_filter != COLOR_FILTER_NONE) {
1687     // Color filter requires color to be enabled
1688     if (opts.color == COLOR_SETTING_FALSE) {
1689       log_error("Error: --color-filter cannot be used with --color=false");
1690       options_config_destroy(config);
1691       SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
1692       return option_error_invalid();
1693     }
1694     // Auto-enable color when color filter is specified
1695     opts.color = COLOR_SETTING_TRUE;
1696     log_debug("Auto-enabled color because --color-filter was provided");
1697   }
1698 
1699   // Detect if splash or status_screen were explicitly set on command line
1700   for (int i = 0; i < mode_argc; i++) {
1701     if (mode_argv[i] &&
1702         (strcmp(mode_argv[i], "--splash-screen") == 0 || strncmp(mode_argv[i], "--splash-screen=", 16) == 0)) {
1703       // Parse the value if provided (--splash-screen=true/false)
1704       bool splash_value = true; // Default to true for plain --splash-screen
1705       if (strncmp(mode_argv[i], "--splash-screen=", 16) == 0) {
1706         const char *value = mode_argv[i] + 16;
1707         if (strcasecmp(value, "false") == 0 || strcasecmp(value, "no") == 0 ||
1708             strcasecmp(value, "0") == 0 || strcasecmp(value, "off") == 0) {
1709           splash_value = false;
1710         }
1711       }
1712       opts.splash_screen = splash_value;
1713       opts.splash_screen_explicitly_set = true;
1714     }
1715     if (mode_argv[i] &&
1716         (strcmp(mode_argv[i], "--status-screen") == 0 || strncmp(mode_argv[i], "--status-screen=", 16) == 0)) {
1717       // Parse the value if provided (--status-screen=true/false)
1718       bool status_value = true; // Default to true for plain --status-screen
1719       if (strncmp(mode_argv[i], "--status-screen=", 16) == 0) {
1720         const char *value = mode_argv[i] + 16;
1721         if (strcasecmp(value, "false") == 0 || strcasecmp(value, "no") == 0 ||
1722             strcasecmp(value, "0") == 0 || strcasecmp(value, "off") == 0) {
1723           status_value = false;
1724         }
1725       }
1726       opts.status_screen = status_value;
1727       opts.status_screen_explicitly_set = true;
1728     }
1729 #ifndef NDEBUG
1730     if (mode_argv[i] &&
1731         (strcmp(mode_argv[i], "--sync-state") == 0 || strncmp(mode_argv[i], "--sync-state=", 13) == 0)) {
1732       opts.debug_sync_state_time_explicit = true;
1733       if (strcmp(mode_argv[i], "--sync-state") == 0) {
1734         if (i + 1 < mode_argc) {
1735           char *endptr;
1736           double val = strtod(mode_argv[i + 1], &endptr);
1737           if (endptr != mode_argv[i + 1] && val > 0.0) {
1738             opts.debug_sync_state_time = val;
1739             i++;
1740           }
1741         }
1742       } else {
1743         const char *value_str = mode_argv[i] + 13;
1744         if (value_str[0] != '\0') {
1745           char *endptr;
1746           double val = strtod(value_str, &endptr);
1747           if (endptr != value_str && val > 0.0) {
1748             opts.debug_sync_state_time = val;
1749           }
1750         }
1751       }
1752     }
1753     if (mode_argv[i] && (strcmp(mode_argv[i], "--backtrace") == 0 || strncmp(mode_argv[i], "--backtrace=", 12) == 0)) {
1754       opts.debug_backtrace_time_explicit = true;
1755       if (strcmp(mode_argv[i], "--backtrace") == 0) {
1756         if (i + 1 < mode_argc) {
1757           char *endptr;
1758           double val = strtod(mode_argv[i + 1], &endptr);
1759           if (endptr != mode_argv[i + 1] && val > 0.0) {
1760             opts.debug_backtrace_time = val;
1761             i++;
1762           }
1763         }
1764       } else {
1765         const char *value_str = mode_argv[i] + 12;
1766         if (value_str[0] != '\0') {
1767           char *endptr;
1768           double val = strtod(value_str, &endptr);
1769           if (endptr != value_str && val > 0.0) {
1770             opts.debug_backtrace_time = val;
1771           }
1772         }
1773       }
1774     }
1775     if (mode_argv[i] &&
1776         (strcmp(mode_argv[i], "--memory-report") == 0 || strncmp(mode_argv[i], "--memory-report=", 16) == 0)) {
1777       opts.debug_memory_report_interval_explicit = true;
1778       if (strcmp(mode_argv[i], "--memory-report") == 0) {
1779         if (i + 1 < mode_argc) {
1780           char *endptr;
1781           double val = strtod(mode_argv[i + 1], &endptr);
1782           if (endptr != mode_argv[i + 1] && val > 0.0) {
1783             opts.debug_memory_report_interval = val;
1784             i++;
1785           }
1786         }
1787       } else {
1788         const char *value_str = mode_argv[i] + 16;
1789         if (value_str[0] != '\0') {
1790           char *endptr;
1791           double val = strtod(value_str, &endptr);
1792           if (endptr != value_str && val > 0.0) {
1793             opts.debug_memory_report_interval = val;
1794           }
1795         }
1796       }
1797     }
1798 #endif
1799   }
1800 
1801   // Auto-disable splash when grep is used (since it's one-time startup screen)
1802   // UNLESS it was explicitly set by the user
1803   // Status screen is now compatible with --grep since we support auto-loading patterns
1804   bool grep_was_provided = false;
1805   for (int i = 0; i < mode_argc; i++) {
1806     if (mode_argv[i] && (strcmp(mode_argv[i], "--grep") == 0 || strncmp(mode_argv[i], "--grep=", 7) == 0)) {
1807       grep_was_provided = true;
1808       break;
1809     }
1810   }
1811 
1812   if (grep_was_provided) {
1813     if (!opts.splash_screen_explicitly_set) {
1814       opts.splash_screen = false;
1815       log_debug("Auto-disabled splash because --grep was provided");
1816     }
1817   }
1818 
1819   // Auto-disable splash and status screens when terminal is non-interactive or CLAUDECODE is set
1820   // This ensures clean output when running under LLM automation or non-interactive shells
1821   bool is_terminal_interactive = terminal_is_interactive();
1822   bool is_claudecode_set = platform_getenv("CLAUDECODE") != NULL;
1823   bool should_auto_disable_screens = !is_terminal_interactive || is_claudecode_set;
1824 
1825   if (should_auto_disable_screens) {
1826     if (!opts.splash_screen_explicitly_set) {
1827       opts.splash_screen = false;
1828       log_debug("Auto-disabled splash (non-interactive terminal or CLAUDECODE set)");
1829     }
1830     if (!opts.status_screen_explicitly_set) {
1831       opts.status_screen = false;
1832       log_debug("Auto-disabled status screen (non-interactive terminal or CLAUDECODE set)");
1833     }
1834   }
1835 
1836   // Validate all string options contain valid UTF-8
1837   // This prevents crashes and corruption from invalid UTF-8 in any option
1838   const char *string_fields[][2] = {{"address", opts.address},
1839                                     {"address6", opts.address6},
1840                                     {"encrypt_key", opts.encrypt_key},
1841                                     {"encrypt_keyfile", opts.encrypt_keyfile},
1842                                     {"server_key", opts.server_key},
1843                                     {"client_keys", opts.client_keys},
1844                                     {"discovery_server", opts.discovery_server},
1845                                     {"discovery_service_key", opts.discovery_service_key},
1846                                     {"discovery_database_path", opts.discovery_database_path},
1847                                     {"log_file", opts.log_file},
1848                                     {"media_file", opts.media_file},
1849                                     {"palette_custom", opts.palette_custom},
1850                                     {"stun_servers", opts.stun_servers},
1851                                     {"turn_servers", opts.turn_servers},
1852                                     {"turn_username", opts.turn_username},
1853                                     {"turn_credential", opts.turn_credential},
1854                                     {"turn_secret", opts.turn_secret},
1855                                     {"session_string", opts.session_string},
1856                                     {NULL, NULL}};
1857 
1858   for (int i = 0; string_fields[i][0] != NULL; i++) {
1859     const char *field_name = string_fields[i][0];
1860     const char *field_value = string_fields[i][1];
1861 
1862     // Skip empty strings
1863     if (!field_value || field_value[0] == '\0') {
1864       continue;
1865     }
1866 
1867     // Validate UTF-8
1868     if (!utf8_is_valid(field_value)) {
1869       log_error("Error: Option --%s contains invalid UTF-8 sequences", field_name);
1870       log_error("       Value: %s", field_value);
1871       options_config_destroy(config);
1872       SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
1873       return option_error_invalid();
1874     }
1875   }
1876   // Validate options
1877   result = validate_options_and_report(config, &opts);
1878   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1879     options_config_destroy(config);
1880     SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
1881     return result;
1882   }
1883   // Check for unexpected remaining arguments
1884   if (remaining_argc > 0) {
1885     log_error("Error: Unexpected arguments after options:");
1886     for (int i = 0; i < remaining_argc; i++) {
1887       log_error("  %s", remaining_argv[i]);
1888     }
1889     options_config_destroy(config);
1890     SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
1891     return option_error_invalid();
1892   }
1893 
1894   // Mode-specific post-processing
1895   // Apply mode-specific defaults (port, websocket-port)
1896   apply_mode_specific_defaults(&opts);
1897   log_dev("Applied mode-specific defaults: port=%d, websocket_port=%d", opts.port, opts.websocket_port);
1898 
1899   if (detected_mode == MODE_DISCOVERY_SERVICE) {
1900     // Set default paths if not specified
1901     if (opts.discovery_database_path[0] == '\0') {
1902       // Database: Try system-wide location first, fall back to user directories
1903       // Preference: /usr/local/var/ascii-chat/ > ~/.local/share/ascii-chat/ > ~/.config/ascii-chat/
1904       char *db_dir = get_discovery_database_dir();
1905       if (!db_dir) {
1906         options_config_destroy(config);
1907         SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
1908         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to get database directory (tried system and user locations)");
1909       }
1910       safe_snprintf(opts.discovery_database_path, sizeof(opts.discovery_database_path), "%sdiscovery.db", db_dir);
1911       SAFE_FREE(db_dir);
1912     }
1913   }
1914 
1915   options_config_destroy(config);
1916 
1917   // ========================================================================
1918   // STAGE 7: Post-Processing & Validation
1919   // ========================================================================
1920 
1921   // Collect multiple --key flags for multi-key support (server/ACDS only)
1922   // This enables servers to load both SSH and GPG keys and select the right one
1923   // during handshake based on what the client expects
1924   if (detected_mode == MODE_SERVER || detected_mode == MODE_DISCOVERY_SERVICE) {
1925     int num_keys = options_collect_identity_keys(&opts, argc, argv);
1926     if (num_keys < 0) {
1927       SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
1928       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Failed to collect identity keys");
1929     }
1930     // num_keys == 0 is OK (no --key flags provided)
1931   }
1932 
1933   // After parsing command line options, update dimensions
1934   // First set any auto dimensions to terminal size, then apply full height logic
1935 
1936   // Clear auto flags if dimensions were explicitly parsed from options
1937   // This must happen before update_dimensions_to_terminal_size to prevent auto-detected
1938   // values from overwriting user-specified dimensions
1939   // Note: width=0 and height=0 are special values meaning "auto-detect", so don't clear auto flags for those
1940   if (opts.width != OPT_WIDTH_DEFAULT && opts.width != 0) {
1941     opts.auto_width = false;
1942   }
1943   if (opts.height != OPT_HEIGHT_DEFAULT && opts.height != 0) {
1944     opts.auto_height = false;
1945   }
1946   update_dimensions_to_terminal_size(&opts);
1947   update_dimensions_for_full_height(&opts);
1948   // Apply verbose level to log threshold
1949   // Each -V decreases the log level by 1 (showing more verbose output)
1950   // Minimum level is LOG_DEV (0)
1951   if (opts.verbose_level > 0) {
1952     log_level_t current_level = log_get_level();
1953     int new_level = (int)current_level - (int)opts.verbose_level;
1954     if (new_level < LOG_DEV) {
1955       new_level = LOG_DEV;
1956     }
1957     log_set_level((log_level_t)new_level);
1958   }
1959 
1960   // Check WEBCAM_DISABLED environment variable to enable test pattern mode
1961   // Useful for CI/CD and testing environments without a physical webcam
1962   const char *webcam_disabled = SAFE_GETENV("WEBCAM_DISABLED");
1963   if (webcam_disabled &&
1964       (strcmp(webcam_disabled, "1") == 0 || platform_strcasecmp(webcam_disabled, "true") == 0 ||
1965        platform_strcasecmp(webcam_disabled, "yes") == 0 || platform_strcasecmp(webcam_disabled, "on") == 0)) {
1966     opts.test_pattern = true;
1967   }
1968 
1969   // Apply --no-compress interaction with audio encoding
1970   if (opts.no_compress) {
1971     opts.encode_audio = false;
1972     log_debug("--no-compress set: disabling audio encoding");
1973   }
1974 
1975   // Set media_from_stdin flag if media_file is "-"
1976   if (opts.media_file[0] != '\0' && strcmp(opts.media_file, "-") == 0) {
1977     opts.media_from_stdin = true;
1978     log_debug("Media file set to stdin");
1979   }
1980 
1981   // Validate --seek option
1982   if (opts.media_seek_timestamp > 0.0) {
1983     // Can't seek stdin
1984     if (opts.media_from_stdin) {
1985       log_error("--seek cannot be used with stdin (--file -)");
1986       SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
1987       return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
1988     }
1989 
1990     // Require --file or --url
1991     if (opts.media_file[0] == '\0' && opts.media_url[0] == '\0') {
1992       log_error("--seek requires --file or --url");
1993       SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
1994       return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
1995     }
1996   }
1997 
1998   // Validate --pause option
1999   if (opts.pause) {
2000     // Require --file or --url (not webcam, not test pattern)
2001     if (opts.media_file[0] == '\0' && opts.media_url[0] == '\0') {
2002       log_error("--pause requires --file or --url");
2003       SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
2004       return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
2005     }
2006   }
2007 
2008   // Validate --url option
2009   if (opts.media_url[0] != '\0') {
2010     // URL must be a valid HTTP(S) URL (YouTube URLs are HTTPS URLs)
2011     if (!url_is_valid(opts.media_url)) {
2012       log_error("--url must be a valid HTTP(S) URL: %s", opts.media_url);
2013       SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
2014       return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
2015     }
2016 
2017     // Normalize bare URLs by prepending http:// if not present
2018     if (!strstr(opts.media_url, "://")) {
2019       char normalized_url[2048];
2020       int result = snprintf(normalized_url, sizeof(normalized_url), "http://%s", opts.media_url);
2021       if (result > 0 && result < (int)sizeof(normalized_url)) {
2022         SAFE_STRNCPY(opts.media_url, normalized_url, sizeof(opts.media_url));
2023       } else {
2024         log_error("Failed to normalize URL (too long): %s", opts.media_url);
2025         SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
2026         return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
2027       }
2028     }
2029   }
2030 
2031   // ========================================================================
2032   // STAGE 7: Publish to RCU
2033   // ========================================================================
2034 
2035   // Save the quiet flag before publishing (RCU will be cleaned up before memory report runs)
2036 #if defined(DEBUG_MEMORY) && !defined(USE_MIMALLOC_DEBUG) && !defined(NDEBUG)
2037   bool quiet_for_memory_report = opts.quiet;
2038 #endif
2039 
2040   // Publish parsed options to RCU state (replaces options_state_populate_from_globals)
2041   // This makes the options visible to all threads via lock-free reads
2042   asciichat_error_t publish_result = options_state_set(&opts);
2043   if (publish_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2044     log_error("Failed to publish parsed options to RCU state: %d", publish_result);
2045     SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
2046     return publish_result;
2047   }
2048 
2049   // Now update debug memory quiet mode with the saved quiet value
2050 #if defined(DEBUG_MEMORY) && !defined(USE_MIMALLOC_DEBUG) && !defined(NDEBUG)
2051   debug_memory_set_quiet_mode(quiet_for_memory_report);
2052 #endif
2053 
2054   // ========================================================================
2055   // Apply color scheme to logging
2056   // ========================================================================
2057   // Now that options are parsed, set and apply the selected color scheme to logging
2058   if (opts.color_scheme_name[0] != '\0') {
2059     asciichat_error_t scheme_result = colorscheme_set_active_scheme(opts.color_scheme_name);
2060     if (scheme_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2061       const color_scheme_t *scheme = colorscheme_get_active_scheme();
2062       if (scheme) {
2063         log_set_color_scheme(scheme);
2064         log_debug("Color scheme applied: %s", opts.color_scheme_name);
2065       }
2066     } else {
2067       log_warn("Failed to apply color scheme: %s", opts.color_scheme_name);
2068     }
2069   }
2070 
2071   // ========================================================================
2072   // STAGE 8: Execute deferred actions (after all options parsed and published)
2073   // ========================================================================
2074   // Deferred actions (--list-webcams, --list-microphones, --list-speakers, --show-capabilities)
2075   // are executed here after all options are fully parsed and published via RCU.
2076   // This ensures action output reflects the final parsed state (e.g., final dimensions
2077   // for --show-capabilities).
2078   // Re-enable terminal output so deferred actions can print their results.
2079   // It was disabled earlier (STAGE 1C) to suppress log noise during option parsing.
2080   if (has_action) {
2081     log_set_terminal_output(true);
2082   }
2083   actions_execute_deferred();
2084   SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
2085   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2086 }

Referenced by config_create_default(),

options_t
  options_t_new_preserve_binary (const options_t * source)


Create new options struct, preserving binary-level fields from
    source. This version of options_t_new() extracts binary-level options
    from a source struct before performing a full reset, then restores them
    after. This allows binary-level options (--quiet, --verbose, --log-level,
    etc.) to persist through multiple reset cycles without manual save/restore
    code.




Parameters


source The options struct to extract binary-level
  fields from (NULL-safe)


Returns


New options_t with defaults and binary-level fields
  preserved


Definition at line 705 of file
    lib/options/options.c.


705                                                                  {
706   // Extract binary-level options before reset
707   binary_level_opts_t binary = extract_binary_level(source);
708 
709   // Create fresh options with all defaults
710   options_t opts = options_t_new();
711 
712   // Restore binary-level options (overwrite defaults)
713   restore_binary_level(&opts, &binary);
714 
715   return opts;
716 }
